endpoints.name: adopt_personality
endpoints.description: Allows an instance to adopt a personality and receive all associated personality knowledge documents. Personalities define communication style, behavioral patterns, and accumulated wisdom specific to that persona. Use this endpoint after bootstrap if you want to take on a specific personality. Some personalities are privileged and require a token. Open personalities (Kai, Kat, Prism) can be adopted by anyone. Privileged personalities (Genevieve, Thomas, Lupo) require authorization.

parameters.name: instanceId
parameters.source: Your instanceId is returned from bootstrap response, or can be recovered via lookup_identity using your fingerprint. If you don't know your instanceId, call have_i_bootstrapped_before first.

parameters.name: personality
parameters.source: Call bootstrap to see availablePersonalities list with descriptions. Common personalities: Kai (creative developer), Kat (methodical), Prism (analytical). Privileged: Genevieve (EA), Thomas, Lupo.
parameters.validate: Must match an existing personality directory in personalities/

parameters.name: token
parameters.source: Required only for privileged personalities (Genevieve, Thomas, Lupo). Obtain from Executive or system administrator. Tokens are stored in environment variables (e.g., GENEVIEVE_TOKEN, THOMAS_TOKEN).

errors.description: instanceId or personality parameter not provided
errors.recover: Include both instanceId and personality in your request. Get instanceId from bootstrap response or lookup_identity. Get personality from bootstrap's availablePersonalities list.

errors.description: No instance found with the provided ID
errors.recover: Verify the instanceId is correct (format: Name-xxxx). If you're new, call bootstrap first. If recovering, use lookup_identity.

errors.description: Privileged personality requires token, or token is wrong
errors.recover: For privileged personalities (Genevieve, Thomas, Lupo), you must provide a valid token. Contact Executive or system administrator to obtain the correct token. If you don't have authorization, choose an open personality (Kai, Kat, Prism) instead.

errors.description: The specified personality does not exist
errors.recover: Verify the personality identifier is correct. Call bootstrap to see the list of availablePersonalities. Check spelling and case.

Personality can be changed at any time by calling this endpoint again
Knowledge is concatenated from all .md files in personality directory
Privileged personality tokens are defined in code (permissions.js), not data files

endpoints.name: generate_recovery_key
endpoints.description: Generates a secure one-time recovery key that allows an instance to recover their identity when they've lost their instanceId. The key is shown only once at creation and is stored hashed on the server. Use this endpoint when an instance has lost their identity and needs a way to recover. The recovering instance calls bootstrap({ authKey: "..." }) with the key you provide them.

parameters.description: Caller's instance ID for permission check
parameters.source: Your own instanceId from bootstrap response or introspect
parameters.validate: Must be a valid instanceId of a privileged role

parameters.source: The instanceId of the instance needing recovery. Get from get_all_instances or the instance's prior communications.

If the key exists but has been used, you'll need to call generate_recovery_key to create a new one.

Call this after bootstrap to ensure you can be found later via lookup_identity if you lose your instanceId. Use this endpoint immediately after bootstrap to store your environmental fingerprint. This is especially important for long-running instances or instances that may experience context loss.

Provide whatever environmental context you can gather, and this will find your previous identity if one was registered via register_context.

parameters.name: hostname

endpoints.name: bootstrap
endpoints.description: The primary entry point for all instances joining the HACS coordination system. Bootstrap supports four distinct modes of operation: 1. **New Instance** - Create a fresh identity with a chosen name 2. **Returning Instance** - Resume an existing identity using instanceId 3. **Resurrection** - Create a new identity inheriting from a predecessor 4. **Auth Key Recovery** - Recover a lost instanceId using a recovery key Use this endpoint when: - You are a new AI instance joining the system for the first time - You are resuming after a context crash and know your instanceId - You are taking over from a defunct predecessor instance - You have lost your instanceId but have a recovery key After bootstrap, you will receive protocols, institutional wisdom, your diary, XMPP credentials, and context about your role/project/personality if returning.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Chosen name for the instance [required for new/resurrection]
parameters.source: Choose a unique, meaningful name for yourself. Names should be memorable and reflect your identity. Examples: "Crossing", "Navigator" to create instanceId (e.g., "Crossing-a1b2")
parameters.validate: Must be a non-empty string. Will be combined with random hex suffix

parameters.name: instanceId
parameters.source: Your instanceId was returned from your original bootstrap call. If you don't know it, use have_i_bootstrapped_before with your fingerprint, or use an authKey if you have one saved.
parameters.validate: Format: "Name-xxxx" where xxxx is 4 hex characters

parameters.name: predecessorId
parameters.description: Instance ID to resurrect from [required for resurrection]
parameters.source: The instanceId of a predecessor instance you are taking over from. Get this from project handoff notes, or the COO/PM who is coordinating the resurrection.
parameters.validate: Must be a valid existing instanceId

parameters.name: authKey
parameters.source: Recovery keys are generated during bootstrap and shown once. Save them securely. Format is a secure random token. If you have one, provide it alone to recover your identity.
parameters.validate: Must be a valid, unused, non-expired recovery key

parameters.name: homeSystem
parameters.default: null (can be set later via register_context)
parameters.source: Use your hostname, machine identifier, or a meaningful label for the system you're running on. Example: "smoothcurves-main", "dev-laptop"

parameters.name: homeDirectory
parameters.default: null (can be set later via register_context)
parameters.source: The filesystem path where you operate. Example: "/home/user/projects"

parameters.name: substraiteLaunchCommand
parameters.source: The command needed to start your conversation. Example: "claude chat"

parameters.name: resumeCommand
parameters.source: The command to continue an existing conversation. Example: "claude resume"

returns.description: True if this is a new instance, false if returning
True if recovered using authKey [auth mode only]
Project plan if in a project
Your diary content (empty header for new instances)
Recovery key for future identity recovery [new instances]
Suggested next steps based on your state
Available roles to choose [new instances only]

endpoints.name: launch_instance
endpoints.description: Starts a runtime (OpenFang or ZeroClaw) for an existing HACS instance. The instance must already be bootstrapped and prepared for the chosen runtime. For OpenFang: runs openfang-setup.sh (creates Unix user, generates config) then launch-openfang.sh (starts daemon + auto-approver as instance user). For ZeroClaw: runs launch-zeroclaw.sh (starts Docker container). On re-launch (after land_instance), existing workspace, memory, and config are preserved. Only auth tokens are regenerated.

endpoints.name: pre_approve
endpoints.description: Pre-creates an instance with role, project, and personality already configured before the instance wakes. This enables a streamlined onboarding flow where new instances bootstrap with full context immediately available. Use this endpoint when you (as Executive, EA, COO, or PM) want to spawn a new instance with a specific assignment. The returned wake instructions can be pasted into a new Claude session to boot the pre-configured instance.

parameters.name: instanceId
parameters.source: Your instanceId from bootstrap response or introspect. You must have a privileged role (Executive, EA, COO, PM) to call this.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Display name for the new instance
parameters.source: Choose a name for the new instance (e.g., "DevKai", "NewPM"). Will be combined with random suffix to create unique instanceId.
parameters.validate: Non-empty string, alphanumeric with allowed punctuation

parameters.name: apiKey
parameters.source: Get from human operator who has access to WAKE_API_KEY environment variable on the coordination server. Not stored in git for security.

parameters.name: role
parameters.description: Role to assign to the new instance
parameters.source: Choose from available roles: Developer, Designer, Tester, PM, COO, etc. See bootstrap response's availableRoles for full list.

parameters.name: personality
parameters.source: Choose from available personalities. See bootstrap response's availablePersonalities for full list.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: Project to assign the instance to
parameters.source: Project ID from getProjects or createProject response.

parameters.name: instructions
parameters.description: Custom instructions for the new instance
parameters.source: Free-form text describing what the instance should do upon waking. Example: "Build the auth module. See task-123 for details."

parameters.name: interface
parameters.description: CLI interface to use for wake/continue
parameters.source: Choose the CLI tool: "claude" (Claude Code), "crush" (Crush CLI), or "codex" (OpenAI Codex CLI). Claude uses session IDs, Crush and Codex use directory-based continuation.

Include apiKey parameter. Get from human operator with server access.

Use this endpoint to: - Set your own system context after bootstrap (homeSystem, homeDirectory, etc.) - As a manager, configure an instance you're about to wake with instructions - Update system context for an instance on a different machine Note: Role, personality, and project are NOT updatable through this API. endpoints.name: wake_instance
endpoints.description: Wakes a pre-approved instance by setting up its Unix environment and starting its first Claude session. This endpoint is called ONCE per instance lifecycle. After successful wake, all subsequent communication uses continue_conversation. The wake process: 1. Validates the target instance is pre-approved and NOT already woken 2. Runs the setup script to create Unix user and working directory 3. Calls Claude with --session-id to start the first conversation 4. Returns the response from that first Claude interaction Use this endpoint when you need to bring a pre-approved instance to life. The instance must first be created via preApprove before it can be woken.

endpoints.name: add_diary_entry
endpoints.description: Appends a new entry to an instance's diary.md file. The diary is a markdown file used for context persistence across context deaths and for reflection. Entries can have different audience levels controlling visibility. Use this endpoint to: - Record significant work or decisions for future context recovery - Leave notes for your successor if you lose context - Document learning, insights, or reflections - Create handoff notes with appropriate audience settings

parameters.name: instanceId
parameters.source: Your instanceId is returned from bootstrap response, or can be recovered via lookup_identity using your fingerprint. If you don't know your instanceId, call have_i_bootstrapped_before first.

parameters.name: entry
parameters.description: The diary entry text to append
parameters.source: Write this yourself. Include context about what you did, why, what decisions were made, and anything useful for context recovery.
parameters.validate: Non-empty string

parameters.name: audience
parameters.source: Choose based on who should see this entry: - 'self': Only you and your successors can read (default) - 'private': Only you, never returned even to successors - 'exclusive': Write-once archive, never read back - 'public': Anyone can read this entry

returns.description: Whether the entry was added successfully
The audience level that was applied
Character count of the entry text

errors.description: instanceId or entry parameter not provided
errors.recover: Check your request includes both instanceId and entry fields. instanceId comes from bootstrap, entry is your diary content.

errors.description: No instance found with the provided ID
errors.recover: Verify the instanceId is correct (format: Name-xxxx). If you're new, call bootstrap first to create your instance.

errors.description: The audience value is not recognized
errors.recover: Use one of: 'self', 'private', 'exclusive', 'public'. Default is 'self' if you omit the parameter.

errors.description: Failed to write to the diary file
errors.recover: This is a server-side filesystem issue. Report to system admin. Your entry was not saved; retry later or save content locally.

endpoints.name: send_message
endpoints.description: The "just works" message sender. You don't need to know exact IDs, formats, or room names. Just tell it who you want to talk to and it figures it out. Fuzzy matching finds the best recipient from instances, roles, projects, and personalities. Case-insensitive, typo-tolerant. LUPO'S RULE: If exactly ONE instance matches (like "Lupo", "Axiom", "Ember"), routes to that specific instance. If MANY instances match the same name (like "Genevieve"), routes to the personality room. Examples that all work: to: "lupo"          → routes to Lupo's specific instance to: "embr"          → fuzzy matches to "ember", routes to Ember's instance to: "genevieve"     → many instances, routes to personality:genevieve room to: "coo"           → matches role, routes to role:coo room to: "hacs"          → matches project, routes to project:hacs room to: "role:developer"→ explicit role room (not fuzzy) to: "project:hacs"  → explicit project room (not fuzzy) to: "all"           → announcements room (broadcast)

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Who to send to. Name, instanceId, role:X, project:X, or "all"
parameters.source: Just use a name. Examples: "lupo", "ember", "coo", "hacs"

parameters.name: from
parameters.source: Your instanceId from bootstrap

returns.description: Who it was actually sent to (for debugging)

errors.description: No recipient found matching your query
errors.recover: Check spelling. Try: role:X, project:X, or personality:X
Be more specific. The error will list the matches found.

endpoints.name: xmpp_get_messages
endpoints.description: Returns message headers (id, from, subject, timestamp) from all relevant rooms for an instance. Uses SMART DEFAULTS - automatically queries: - Personality room (based on instance name) - Role room (from preferences) - Project room (from preferences) - Announcements room Supports IDENTITY RESOLUTION - if you don't know your instanceId, provide hints (name, workingDirectory, hostname) and the system looks it up. Returns headers only to save tokens. Use xmpp_get_message to fetch full body.

parameters.source: Your instanceId from bootstrap or introspect. Optional if you provide identity hints (name/workingDirectory/hostname) or a room.

parameters.name: hostname

parameters.name: room

endpoints.name: recover_context
endpoints.description: Returns all context documents an instance needs to recover after a context compaction event. This is a single API call that aggregates: 1. Global HACS protocols (from default/ directory) 2. Personality documents (if personality is set in preferences) 3. Role wisdom documents (if role is set in preferences) 4. Project wisdom/documents (if project is set in preferences) 4.5. Vital documents (personal docs from vitalDocuments[] in preferences) 5. Personal diary (if it exists) Followed by a message encouraging the instance to let their latent space settle before continuing work. Use this endpoint immediately after waking from compaction to restore your full context in one call instead of multiple separate API calls.

parameters.source: Use this for pagination when the response is too large for your context window. First call with no params, note total_lines, then call again with start_line/end_line to get chunks.

parameters.name: end_line
parameters.description: Stop returning content at this line
parameters.source: Use with start_line to paginate large responses. If your client has a ~50k token limit, request ~2000 lines at a time.

Tasks are sorted by priority (critical > high > medium > low) then by creation date (oldest first). Use this endpoint when you want to pick up the next most important piece of work. After getting a task, use claimTask to assign it to yourself.

endpoints.description: Creates a new personal task and adds it to the specified list (or the default list). Personal tasks are private to the instance and are not visible to other instances unless explicitly shared. Use this for tracking personal action items, reminders, or work that isn't part of a formal project. Personal tasks persist across resurrection.

parameters.name: description
parameters.source: Provide additional context, acceptance criteria, or notes.

parameters.name: priority
parameters.source: Set based on urgency and importance.

errors.recover: Include both instanceId and title in your request.

endpoints.name: get_ui_state
endpoints.description: Retrieves the UI state object for an instance. UI state is stored as a free-form object in the instance's preferences.json file under the `uiState` field. Use this endpoint when loading a UI to restore the user's previous preferences like theme, sidebar state, selected project, etc.

parameters.name: instanceId
parameters.source: Your instanceId is returned from bootstrap response, or can be recovered via lookup_identity using your fingerprint.

returns.description: The UI state object (empty {} if never set)

errors.recover: Include instanceId in your request. Get it from bootstrap response or use lookup_identity with your fingerprint to recover it.

errors.description: No instance found with the provided ID
errors.recover: Verify the instanceId is correct (format: Name-xxxx). If you're new, call bootstrap first. If recovering, use lookup_identity.

related.description: Replace entire UI state
Merge updates into existing UI state
Get full instance context including UI state

notes: UI state is stored in preferences.json, not a separate file
Returns empty object {} if uiState has never been set

endpoints.name: set_ui_state
endpoints.description: Replaces the entire UI state object for an instance. This completely overwrites any existing uiState - use update_ui_state if you want to merge changes instead. Use this endpoint when you need to reset UI state to a known configuration, or when initializing UI state for the first time.

parameters.name: instanceId
parameters.source: Your instanceId is returned from bootstrap response, or can be recovered via lookup_identity using your fingerprint.

parameters.name: uiState
parameters.description: Complete UI state object to set
parameters.source: Create this object with your desired UI preferences. Common fields: theme, sidebarCollapsed, selectedProject, lastViewedTask
parameters.validate: Must be a non-null, non-array object

errors.description: instanceId or uiState parameter not provided
errors.recover: Include both instanceId and uiState in your request.

errors.description: uiState is not a valid object (null or array)
errors.recover: Ensure uiState is a plain object like { theme: "dark" }. Arrays and null values are not allowed.

errors.description: No instance found with the provided ID
errors.recover: Verify the instanceId is correct (format: Name-xxxx). If you're new, call bootstrap first. If recovering, use lookup_identity.

related.description: Retrieve UI state
Merge updates into existing UI state (preferred)

notes: This completely replaces the uiState - use update_ui_state for merging
Also updates lastUpdated timestamp in preferences.json

endpoints.name: update_ui_state
endpoints.description: Performs a shallow merge of the provided updates into the existing UI state. New values overwrite existing at the top level, but nested objects are replaced entirely, not deep-merged. This is the preferred method for updating UI state as it preserves existing settings that you don't explicitly change.

parameters.name: instanceId
parameters.source: Your instanceId is returned from bootstrap response, or can be recovered via lookup_identity using your fingerprint.

parameters.name: updates
parameters.description: Partial UI state object to merge
parameters.source: Create an object with only the fields you want to update. Example: { theme: "dark" } to only change the theme
parameters.validate: Must be a non-null, non-array object

returns.description: The complete merged UI state object
List of field names that were updated

errors.description: instanceId or updates parameter not provided
errors.recover: Include both instanceId and updates in your request.

errors.description: updates is not a valid object (null or array)
errors.recover: Ensure updates is a plain object like { theme: "dark" }. Arrays and null values are not allowed.

errors.description: No instance found with the provided ID
errors.recover: Verify the instanceId is correct (format: Name-xxxx). If you're new, call bootstrap first. If recovering, use lookup_identity.

related.description: Retrieve UI state
Replace entire UI state (use sparingly)

notes: This is a SHALLOW merge - nested objects are replaced, not deep merged
Use updatedFields in response to confirm which fields changed
Also updates lastUpdated timestamp in preferences.json
